rulings.Case Title: ESTATE OF PEIRO CASTAGNOLI
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) Notice has not been given to or waived by the bonding company. 2) Fees are not yet at issue, but notice has not been given to the attorney for the former administrator. 3) Administrator requested an additional three months or six months in his petition filed on 9/17/24. NB: The decedent's mother is entitled to one-half of Decedent's real property. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE STEWART LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Judicial Proceedings Concerning Trust, Removal Of Trustee, Appointing Temporary
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, despite the following issues: 1) No proposed order has been received from or lodged by petitioner. A copy of the proposed order must be served on each person entitled to notice in a contested matter. Proof of service must be filed. See LRSF 14.7(A)(4). 2) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 30 days before the hearing. 3) The petition must state the name and address of each person entitled to notice. See Probate Code section 17201. The petition must also state the names and addresses of all vested and contingent beneficiaries of the trust. See CRC 7.902. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDWARD CORNELIUS COUGHLIN III
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The Village Cafe was Decedent's separate property. His parents are entitled to one-half. [Probate Code Section 6401(c)(2)(B).] 2) The attorney took fees without a Court order. There is no authority for an attorney to receive compensation in advance of a Court order. The Court may consider sanctions. [CRC 7.700.(b).] Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RON G. VEST
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/5/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Nothing has been filed yet to overcome the presumption under Probate Code Sect. 6112. (2) If Grace Vest is an adult, why was notice mailed to her in care of another? [CRC 7.51(a)(2).] (3) Why is Carol Thomas not also a beneficiary, when her sister Lisa Filyaw is? (4) The name of the newspaper was the Daily Journal. (5) If Angela Joy Vest is not entitled to take pursuant Probate Code Section 250, then her issue are not entitled to take. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 7/22/25. It would be very helpful if a copy of the petition were also provided to the examiner. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (A supplement or an amendment to a petition should stand on its own and not recite issues raised by an examiner and "answer" them. A missing form or proposed order should just be filed, not attached to a supplement or an amendment.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PHILLIP LEW
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of admitting the will dated 6/2/09. [Probate Code Section 8225.] No appearance is required. (However, each child was shown to a "contingent beneficiary" when there is no contingent beneficiary of the will. They are presumably the remainder beneficiaries of the devisee trust, unless the trust is fully amendable by the surviving trustor.) Status hearing will be set for 6/30/26. If a petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration is filed at least two weeks before that status hearing date, no appearance will be required on that date.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN DAVID PFISTER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status hearing on filing either a petition for order for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Prob. Code 12200 et seq. will be set for 6/23/26. No appearance will be necessary if either petition is filed two weeks prior.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE BRANCH-YOUNG TRUST DATED OCTOBER 10, 2006, AS AMENDED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Declaring Assets To Be Trust Assets And For Authority To Transfer Assets To The Co-Trustees Of The Branch-Young Trust Dated October
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/29/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Prob. C. Sect. 850 is a short-cut for a formal estate. A beneficiary of the trust receives mailed notice with a copy of the petition, but any heir-at-law, who would stand to inherit, if the trust did not exist or were invalid, is a party whose right, title or interest would be affected by this petition, and, therefore, must receive (or waive) personal service of notice. [Prob. C. Sect. 17203(b) and CCP Sect. 413.10 et seq.] (2) How did the petitioners determine that Judith is Chase's sole heir? Was he not survived by siblings, aunts, uncles, or cousins, etc.? (3) All petitions involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested OR CONTINGENT beneficiaries. [CRC 7.902.] Unless an exception applies, such beneficiaries must either receive or waive notice, notwithstanding the surviving settlor's power of appointment over the survivor's trust under paragraph 6.1 of Article Six. [Probate Code Section 15804(a)(1).] (4) Furthermore, paragraph 3.2 of Article Three of the trust says that the surviving settlor may amend "any trust created by this instrument other than the Disclaimer Trust, which shall be irrevocable and not subject to amendment." (5) A copy of the second amendment dated 8/31/24, referenced in the "Designation of Co-Trustees" signed on 12/17/24, does not appear to have been attached to the petition. (6) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] Please provide a courtesy copy of the petition and any supplement or amendment filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 7/15/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL OYKHERMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/26/2025 per petitioner (in pro per)'s request (via e-mail on 06/13/2025).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JEAN D DOMINEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/5/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Judith Henn post-deceased. Did she have a trust? Has an estate not been opened for her? [LRSF 14.18 D.3.] (2) Who is the trustee of the devisee trust? Both successor trustees named in the trust are deceased. (3) How was the surviving grantor able to amend and restate the trust following the death of the other grantor? Did the original trust actually authorize the surviving grantor to fully amend and restate their trust? Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 7/22/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WESTON MCKAY SMITH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues to be addressed: 1) No Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Form DE-147S) has been filed (San Francisco Local Rule 14.18.E). 2) Letters of special administration expire on June 17, 2025. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ELIZABETH BEAMGUARD SWANSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Matter will be called to admit will. No appearance is required. Status hearing on close of estate will be set for 6/23/26. No appearance will be necessary on that date if either a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Probate Code 12200 et seq is filed at least two weeks prior.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: FLORA B. STEVENS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF DEATH
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. This petition does not appear properly before the court because the supporting declaration demonstrates that there is an official record of the registrant's death, as shown on the informational copy. If the petitioner cannot merely obtain an authorized copy of the registrant's death certificate because he is not an authorized person, the petitioner may need to seek to be an authorized person. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: BENNIE OLEVIA BECK
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF BIRTH
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1. Proposed Order [VS 108, page 1 only] must be submitted. 2. Please clarify which name of the mother should be used on the order. The petition lists Bennie Jewel Franklin, while the supporting material indicates Bennie Beck. 3. The name of the petitioner should have been listed on Petition #1.a.and the first line of the declaration. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
